stemHtml: The operator of an aircraft equipped with 50 seats uses standard masses for passengers and baggage. During the preparation of a scheduled flight a group of passengers present themselves at the check-in desk, it is apparent that even the lightest of these exceeds the value of the declared standard mass.

options.a: The operator may use the standard masses for the balance but must correct these for the load calculation.
options.b: The operator is obliged to use the actual masses of each passenger.
options.c: The operator may use the standard masses for the load and balance calculation without correction.
options.d: The operator must determine the actual mass of such passengers by weighing or by adding an adequate increment.
